channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Ellison Family’s Expanding Influence on Global Media
item.description: The Ellison family, led by David Ellison, is rapidly emerging as a major player in the global media landscape. Following their 2025 merger of Skydance Media with Paramount Global, which granted them c...

item.title: Report of Tiger Poaching and Buried Carcass in Middle East Amid War
item.description: The news reports a sensational claim regarding a tiger in the Middle East that was allegedly poached and its carcass buried, with the story surfacing 23 days later. Authorities are reportedly investig...

item.title: Police search for driver in Lethbridge hit-and-run incident
item.description: Police in Lethbridge, Alberta, are actively searching for a driver involved in a hit-and-run incident. The crash took place recently, and authorities are urging anyone with information to come forward...

item.title: Artemis II Mission Approaches Moon, Set to Surpass Apollo 13 Distance Record
item.description: The Artemis II mission, NASA's first crewed test flight under the Artemis program, is making history as it approaches the moon. Launched on April 1 from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida, the spacec...

item.title: US and Iran Receive Peace Proposal Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have received a framework for a potential peace agreement, aiming to end the ongoing hostilities between the two countries. The proposal comes amid escalating tensions in th...

item.title: How Australia’s pedestrian button became a timeless icon of inclusive design
item.description: The PB/5 pedestrian crossing button, introduced in 1984 by the New South Wales Department of Main Roads, has become one of Australia’s most enduring and recognisable urban sounds. Recently inducted in...

item.title: Toys 'R' Us Canada defends its trademarks in multiple legal disputes
item.description: Toys 'R' Us Canada is engaged in several active legal and administrative disputes over the protection of its trademarks, marking a significant phase in the brand’s post-bankruptcy era. The company, no...

item.title: Police Prevent Kidnap, Arrest Suspect, Recover Firearms in Port Harcourt
item.description: The Rivers State Police have achieved a major success in their fight against kidnapping following the foiling of a kidnap attempt in Port Harcourt. On March 20, a 35-year-old man, Fadi Nasser, narrowl...

item.title: Makinde Expresses Concern Over Threats to Nigeria's Democracy
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, don raise alarm say Nigeria fit dey waka towards one-party system if care no dey. For him newsletter, 'The Business of Governance', e talk say some recent moves for m...

item.title: Two Scorpio SUVs involved in collision in Gurugram amid road rage incident
item.description: In a recent incident in Gurugram, two Scorpio SUVs collided with each other during a road rage episode, causing alarm among local commuters. The event was captured on a video which quickly spread acro...

item.title: Georgia Men's Golf Team Finishes Tied for Fifth at Augusta Haskins Award Invitational
item.description: The University of Georgia men's golf team finished tied for fifth at the Augusta Haskins Award Invitational with an impressive 28-under par after the third round. Junior Grayson Wood led the team with...

item.title: Iran seeks permanent peace as Trump issues final warning over Hormuz blockade
item.description: Iran has rejected a new 45‑day ceasefire plan proposed by mediators, insisting instead on a permanent end to the war, as tensions with the United States escalate over the closure of the Strait of Horm...

item.title: UNDP and Tanzanian insurers collaborate to strengthen agricultural insurance
item.description: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in collaboration with the Association of Tanzania Insurers (ATI) has launched a new initiative to strengthen agricultural insurance in Tanzania. This su...

item.title: Ex-Nigerian Army Nurse Accuses Senior Officer of Sexual Harassment and Unlawful Detention
item.description: Matilda Anighoro, wey be former nurse for Nigerian Army Medical Corps, don cry out say she suffer sexual harassment, torture, and detention from her oga dem for work. According to her, wahala start fo...

item.title: Innovative Water Extraction Technology Drives Sustainable Mining Practices
item.description: The article highlights how growing global awareness of water scarcity and sustainability challenges has prompted a shift toward responsible water management in the mining sector. Wheaton Precious Meta...

item.title: Israeli airstrike east of Beirut kills Christian political official amid rising tensions
item.description: An Israeli airstrike on an apartment in Ain Saadeh, a predominantly Christian town east of Beirut, has killed a local official from a Christian political party and two women, escalating internal tensi...

item.title: Eastern Cape police rearrest one of eight detainees who escaped over the weekend
item.description: Eastern Cape authorities successfully rearrested one of eight detainees who escaped from Afsondering police station cells over the weekend. Siphelele Mkhulisi, originally in custody for a murder case,...

item.title: Fresh Violence Hits Jos North As Residents Clash Despite Partial Curfew
item.description: Wahala don burst again for Plateau State as fresh fight jam residents of Anguwan Rukuba in Jos North Local Government Area. The matter start early Wednesday morning, just few hours after government co...

item.title: Trump Rejects Ceasefire Proposal Amid Continued Tensions with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump has dismissed a ceasefire proposal drafted by intermediary countries as 'not good enough' while Iran also rejected a temporary truce. Tensions between the US and Iran remain ...

item.title: Rescued Bats Released in Ukraine Following Winter Relief Efforts
item.description: Following a particularly harsh winter that significantly impacted Ukraine, wildlife rescuers are finding a heartwarming moment of joy in releasing hundreds of bats back into the wild. These bats were ...
